commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `normalizeE164` to return `""` for no-digit input and adds unit coverage for valid and no-digit phone-like strings.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +20. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main returns a bare `+` for no-digit input and `deriveSessionKey` treats that as truthy instead of falling back to `unknown`. I did not run a runtime command in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the SDK behavior change, not a concrete code repair for ClawSweeper to make.

target.title: fix(utils): normalizeE164 returns "" when there are no digits
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

An inbound `From` that contains no digits normalizes to a stray `"+"`. Two real cases hit this:

- a blocked caller that the carrier reports as a literal `Anonymous`
- a bare scheme like `sms://` with no number attached

`"+"` is not a valid E.164 number, and because it is truthy, `deriveSessionKey`'s `from || "unknown"` fallback never fires. Those messages get keyed to a junk `"+"` session bucket instead of the intended `"unknown"`, so anything that special-cases the unknown sender silently skips them.

## Why This Change Was Made

`normalizeE164` only ever returned `` `+${digits}` ``, so empty `digits` produced a bare `"+"`. The fix returns `""` when there is nothing to normalize — which is exactly the value the internal caller already treats as "fall back to unknown". Valid numbers go through the same path as before.

## User Impact

Anonymous / unidentified senders now collapse to the intended `"unknown"` session bucket instead of a bogus `"+"` one, so unknown-sender handling and session bookkeeping behave as designed. No change for normal phone numbers.

## Evidence

Standalone before/after of the function (deterministic string logic):

```
input                 before   after
"+1 (555) 234-5678"   +15552345678   +15552345678   (valid input unchanged)
"tel:+1-555-234-5678" +15552345678   +15552345678   (valid input unchanged)
"Anonymous"           +              ""
"sms://"              +              ""
"+"                   +              ""
```

Through `deriveSessionKey`, `From: "Anonymous"` then goes from session key `"+"` to `"unknown"`.

Added a `normalizeE164` unit test in `src/utils.test.ts` covering both valid numbers and the no-digit cases; it fails on the old behavior and passes with this change (runs in CI). I verified the logic via the standalone repro above rather than the full monorepo suite.
target.author: he-yufeng
